    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "widget",
      "description": "A React progress bar component built on Radix UI primitives that displays visual progress indicators with smooth transitions",
      "file_path": "web/src/components/ui/progress.tsx",
      "functions": [
        "Progress"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [],
      "name": "progress.tsx",
      "source_summary": "import * as React from \"react\";\nimport * as ProgressPrimitive from \"@radix-ui/react-progress\";\n\nimport { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\";\n\nfunction Progress({\n  className,\n  value,\n  ...props\n}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>) {\n  return (\n    <ProgressPrimitive.Root\n      data-slot=\"progress\"\n      className={cn(\n        \"bg-primary/20 relative h-2 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full\",\n        className,\n      )}\n      {...props}\n    >\n      <ProgressPrimitive.Indicator\n        data-slot=\"progress-indicator\"\n        className=\"bg-primary h-full w-full flex-1 transition-all\"\n        style={{ transform: `translateX(-${100 - (value || 0)}%)` }}\n      />\n    </ProgressPrimitive.Root>\n  );\n}\n\nexport { Progress };\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 29,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 1
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "library",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 1,
        "name": "react",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "library",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 2,
        "name": "@radix-ui/react-progress",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "utility",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 4,
        "name": "@/lib/utils",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This is a React functional component that implements a customizable progress bar using Radix UI's progress primitive. The component accepts a `value` prop representing the progress percentage (0-100) and renders a visual indicator that smoothly transitions based on the progress value. It features a rounded bar design with a primary color indicator that moves from left to right as the progress increases. The component is styled using Tailwind CSS classes and supports custom className overrides for additional styling.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Main progress bar component function",
        "interface_type": "function",
        "name": "Progress",
        "parameters": [
          {
            "description": "Custom CSS classes for styling overrides",
            "is_optional": true,
            "name": "className",
            "param_type": "string"
          },
          {
            "description": "Progress percentage value (0-100)",
            "is_optional": true,
            "name": "value",
            "param_type": "number"
          },
          {
            "description": "Additional React props passed to ProgressPrimitive.Root",
            "is_optional": true,
            "name": "props",
            "param_type": "React.ComponentProps"
          }
        ],
        "return_type": "JSX.Element",
        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Render visual progress indicators with smooth animation",
      "Handle progress value transformation and display",
      "Provide customizable styling through className prop",
      "Maintain accessibility through Radix UI primitives",
      "Support responsive progress bar behavior"
    ]
  },
  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "widget",
      "description": "CSS theme component providing syntax highlighting and visual styling for code diffs and comments using Prism.js",
      "file_path": "web/src/components/ui/diff/theme.css",
      "functions": [
        "prism-syntax-highlighting",
        "theme-switching",
        "diff-highlighting",
        "comment-highlighting"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [],
      "name": "theme.css",
      "source_summary": ":root {\n  /**\n * One Light theme for prism.js\n * Based on Atom's One Light theme: https://github.com/atom/atom/tree/master/packages/one-light-syntax\n */\n\n  /**\n * One Light colours (accurate as of commit eb064bf on 19 Feb 2021)\n * From colors.less\n * --mono-1: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n * --mono-2: hsl(230, 6%, 44%);\n * --mono-3: hsl(230, 4%, 64%)\n * --hue-1: hsl(198, 99%, 37%);\n * --hue-2: hsl(221, 87%, 60%);\n * --hue-3: hsl(301, 63%, 40%);\n * --hue-4: hsl(119, 34%, 47%);\n * --hue-5: hsl(5, 74%, 59%);\n * --hue-5-2: hsl(344, 84%, 43%);\n * --hue-6: hsl(35, 99%, 36%);\n * --hue-6-2: hsl(35, 99%, 40%);\n * --syntax-fg: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n * --syntax-bg: hsl(230, 1%, 98%);\n * --syntax-gutter: hsl(230, 1%, 62%);\n * --syntax-guide: hsla(230, 8%, 24%, 0.2);\n * --syntax-accent: hsl(230, 100%, 66%);\n * From syntax-variables.less\n * --syntax-selection-color: hsl(230, 1%, 90%);\n * --syntax-gutter-background-color-selected: hsl(230, 1%, 90%);\n * --syntax-cursor-line: hsla(230, 8%, 24%, 0.05);\n */\n\n  code[class*=\"language-\"],\n  pre[class*=\"language-\"] {\n    background: hsl(230, 1%, 98%);\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n    font-family:\n      \"Fira Code\", \"Fira Mono\", Menlo, Consolas, \"DejaVu Sans Mono\", monospace;\n    direction: ltr;\n    text-align: left;\n    white-space: pre;\n    word-spacing: normal;\n    word-break: normal;\n    line-height: 1.5;\n    -moz-tab-size: 2;\n    -o-tab-size: 2;\n    tab-size: 2;\n    -webkit-hyphens: none;\n    -moz-hyphens: none;\n    -ms-hyphens: none;\n    hyphens: none;\n  }\n\n  /* Selection */\n  code[class*=\"language-\"]::-moz-selection,\n  code[class*=\"language-\"] *::-moz-selection,\n  pre[class*=\"language-\"] *::-moz-selection {\n    background: hsl(230, 1%, 90%);\n    color: inherit;\n  }\n\n  code[class*=\"language-\"]::selection,\n  code[class*=\"language-\"] *::selection,\n  pre[class*=\"language-\"] *::selection {\n    background: hsl(230, 1%, 90%);\n    color: inherit;\n  }\n\n  /* Code blocks */\n  pre[class*=\"language-\"] {\n    padding: 1em;\n    margin: 0.5em 0;\n    overflow: auto;\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n  }\n\n  /* Inline code */\n  :not(pre) > code[class*=\"language-\"] {\n    padding: 0.2em 0.3em;\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n    white-space: normal;\n  }\n\n  .token.comment,\n  .token.prolog,\n  .token.cdata {\n    color: hsl(230, 4%, 64%);\n  }\n\n  .token.doctype,\n  .token.punctuation,\n  .token.entity {\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n  }\n\n  .token.attr-name,\n  .token.class-name,\n  .token.boolean,\n  .token.constant,\n  .token.number,\n  .token.atrule {\n    color: hsl(35, 99%, 36%);\n  }\n\n  .token.keyword {\n    color: hsl(301, 63%, 40%);\n  }\n\n  .token.property,\n  .token.tag,\n  .token.symbol,\n  .token.deleted,\n  .token.important {\n    color: hsl(5, 74%, 59%);\n  }\n\n  .token.selector,\n  .token.string,\n  .token.char,\n  .token.builtin,\n  .token.inserted,\n  .token.regex,\n  .token.attr-value,\n  .token.attr-value > .token.punctuation {\n    color: hsl(119, 34%, 47%);\n  }\n\n  ins,\n  del {\n    text-decoration: none;\n  }\n\n  .token.variable,\n  .token.operator,\n  .token.function {\n    color: hsl(221, 87%, 60%);\n  }\n\n  .token.url {\n    color: hsl(198, 99%, 37%);\n  }\n\n  /* HTML overrides */\n  .token.attr-value > .token.punctuation.attr-equals,\n  .token.special-attr > .token.attr-value > .token.value.css {\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n  }\n\n  /* CSS overrides */\n  .language-css .token.selector {\n    color: hsl(5, 74%, 59%);\n  }\n\n  .language-css .token.property {\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n  }\n\n  .language-css .token.function,\n  .language-css .token.url > .token.function {\n    color: hsl(198, 99%, 37%);\n  }\n\n  .language-css .token.url > .token.string.url {\n    color: hsl(119, 34%, 47%);\n  }\n\n  .language-css .token.important,\n  .language-css .token.atrule .token.rule {\n    color: hsl(301, 63%, 40%);\n  }\n\n  /* JS overrides */\n  .language-javascript .token.operator {\n    color: hsl(301, 63%, 40%);\n  }\n\n  .language-javascript\n    .token.template-string\n    > .token.interpolation\n    > .token.interpolation-punctuation.punctuation {\n    color: hsl(344, 84%, 43%);\n  }\n\n  /* JSON overrides */\n  .language-json .token.operator {\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n  }\n\n  .language-json .token.null.keyword {\n    color: hsl(35, 99%, 36%);\n  }\n\n  /* MD overrides */\n  .language-markdown .token.url,\n  .language-markdown .token.url > .token.operator,\n  .language-markdown .token.url-reference.url > .token.string {\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n  }\n\n  .language-markdown .token.url > .token.content {\n    color: hsl(221, 87%, 60%);\n  }\n\n  .language-markdown .token.url > .token.url,\n  .language-markdown .token.url-reference.url {\n    color: hsl(198, 99%, 37%);\n  }\n\n  .language-markdown .token.blockquote.punctuation,\n  .language-markdown .token.hr.punctuation {\n    color: hsl(230, 4%, 64%);\n    font-style: italic;\n  }\n\n  .language-markdown .token.code-snippet {\n    color: hsl(119, 34%, 47%);\n  }\n\n {\n    color: hsl(5, 74%, 59%);\n  }\n\n  /* General */\n  .token.bold {\n    font-weight: bold;\n  }\n\n  .token.comment,\n  .token.italic {\n    font-style: italic;\n  }\n\n  .token.entity {\n    cursor: help;\n  }\n\n  .token.namespace {\n    opacity: 0.8;\n  }\n\n  /* Plugin overrides */\n  /* Selectors should have higher specificity than those in the plugins' default stylesheets */\n\n  /* Show Invisibles plugin overrides */\n  .token.token.tab:not(:empty):before,\n  .token.token.cr:before,\n  .token.token.lf:before,\n  .token.token.space:before {\n    color: hsla(230, 8%, 24%, 0.2);\n  }\n\n  /* Toolbar plugin overrides */\n  /* Space out all buttons and move them away from the right edge of the code block */\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item {\n    margin-right: 0.4em;\n  }\n\n  /* Styling the buttons */\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > button,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > a,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > span {\n    background: hsl(230, 1%, 90%);\n    color: hsl(230, 6%, 44%);\n    padding: 0.1em 0.4em;\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n  }\n\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > button:hover,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > button:focus,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > a:hover,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > a:focus,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > span:hover,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > span:focus {\n    background: hsl(230, 1%, 78%); /* custom: darken(--syntax-bg, 20%) */\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n  }\n\n  /* Line Highlight plugin overrides */\n  /* The highlighted line itself */\n  .line-highlight.line-highlight {\n    background: hsla(230, 8%, 24%, 0.05);\n  }\n\n  /* Default line numbers in Line Highlight plugin */\n  .line-highlight.line-highlight:before,\n  .line-highlight.line-highlight[data-end]:after {\n    background: hsl(230, 1%, 90%);\n    color: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n    padding: 0.1em 0.6em;\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n    box-shadow: 0 2px 0 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2); /* same as Toolbar plugin default */\n  }\n\n  /* Hovering over a linkable line number (in the gutter area) */\n  /* Requires Line Numbers plugin as well */\n  pre[id].linkable-line-numbers.linkable-line-numbers\n    span.line-numbers-rows\n    > span:hover:before {\n    background-color: hsla(230, 8%, 24%, 0.05);\n  }\n\n  /* Line Numbers and Command Line plugins overrides */\n  /* Line separating gutter from coding area */\n  .line-numbers.line-numbers .line-numbers-rows,\n  .command-line .command-line-prompt {\n    border-right-color: hsla(230, 8%, 24%, 0.2);\n  }\n\n  /* Stuff in the gutter */\n  .line-numbers .line-numbers-rows > span:before,\n  .command-line .command-line-prompt > span:before {\n    color: hsl(230, 1%, 62%);\n  }\n\n  /* Match Braces plugin overrides */\n  /* Note: Outline colour is inherited from the braces */\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-1,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-5,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-9 {\n    color: hsl(5, 74%, 59%);\n  }\n\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-2,\n }\n\n  /* Previewers plugin overrides */\n  /* Based on https://github.com/atom-community/atom-ide-datatip/blob/master/styles/atom-ide-datatips.less and https://github.com/atom/atom/blob/master/packages/one-light-ui */\n  /* Border around popup */\n  .prism-previewer.prism-previewer:before,\n  .prism-previewer-gradient.prism-previewer-gradient div {\n    border-color: hsl(0, 0, 95%);\n  }\n\n  /* Angle and time should remain as circles and are hence not included */\n  .prism-previewer-color.prism-previewer-color:before,\n  .prism-previewer-gradient.prism-previewer-gradient div,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing:before {\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n  }\n\n  /* Triangles pointing to the code */\n  .prism-previewer.prism-previewer:after {\n    border-top-color: hsl(0, 0, 95%);\n  }\n\n  .prism-previewer-flipped.prism-previewer-flipped.after {\n    border-bottom-color: hsl(0, 0, 95%);\n  }\n\n  /* Background colour within the popup */\n  .prism-previewer-angle.prism-previewer-angle:before,\n  .prism-previewer-time.prism-previewer-time:before,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing {\n    background: hsl(0, 0%, 100%);\n  }\n\n  /* For angle, this is the positive area (eg. 90deg will display one quadrant in this colour) */\n  /* For time, this is the alternate colour */\n  .prism-previewer-angle.prism-previewer-angle circle,\n  .prism-previewer-time.prism-previewer-time circle {\n    stroke: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n    stroke-opacity: 1;\n  }\n\n  /* Stroke colours of the handle, direction point, and vector itself */\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing circle,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing path,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing line {\n    stroke: hsl(230, 8%, 24%);\n  }\n\n  /* Fill colour of the handle */\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing circle {\n    fill: transparent;\n  }\n}\n\n.dark {\n  /**\n * One Dark theme for prism.js\n * Based on Atom's One Dark theme: https://github.com/atom/atom/tree/master/packages/one-dark-syntax\n */\n\n  /**\n * One Dark {\n    color: hsl(355, 65%, 65%);\n  }\n\n  /* General */\n  .token.bold {\n    font-weight: bold;\n  }\n\n  .token.comment,\n  .token.italic {\n    font-style: italic;\n  }\n\n  .token.entity {\n    cursor: help;\n  }\n\n  .token.namespace {\n    opacity: 0.8;\n  }\n\n  /* Plugin overrides */\n  /* Selectors should have higher specificity than those in the plugins' default stylesheets */\n\n  /* Show Invisibles plugin overrides */\n  .token.token.tab:not(:empty):before,\n  .token.token.cr:before,\n  .token.token.lf:before,\n  .token.token.space:before {\n    color: hsla(220, 14%, 71%, 0.15);\n    text-shadow: none;\n  }\n\n  /* Toolbar plugin overrides */\n  /* Space out all buttons and move them away from the right edge of the code block */\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item {\n    margin-right: 0.4em;\n  }\n\n  /* Styling the buttons */\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > button,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > a,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > span {\n    background: hsl(220, 13%, 26%);\n    color: hsl(220, 9%, 55%);\n    padding: 0.1em 0.4em;\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n  }\n\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > button:hover,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > button:focus,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > a:hover,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > a:focus,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > span:hover,\n  div.code-toolbar > .toolbar.toolbar > .toolbar-item > span:focus {\n    background: hsl(220, 13%, 28%);\n    color: hsl(220, 14%, 71%);\n  }\n\n  /* Line Highlight plugin overrides */\n  /* The highlighted line itself */\n  .line-highlight.line-highlight {\n    background: hsla(220, 100%, 80%, 0.04);\n  }\n\n  /* Default line numbers in Line Highlight plugin */\n  .line-highlight.line-highlight:before,\n  .line-highlight.line-highlight[data-end]:after {\n    background: hsl(220, 13%, 26%);\n    color: hsl(220, 14%, 71%);\n    padding: 0.1em 0.6em;\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n    box-shadow: 0 2px 0 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2); /* same as Toolbar plugin default */\n  }\n\n  /* Hovering over a linkable line number (in the gutter area) */\n  /* Requires Line Numbers plugin as well */\n  pre[id].linkable-line-numbers.linkable-line-numbers\n    span.line-numbers-rows\n    > span:hover:before {\n    background-color: hsla(220, 100%, 80%, 0.04);\n  }\n\n  /* Line Numbers and Command Line plugins overrides */\n  /* Line separating gutter from coding area */\n  .line-numbers.line-numbers .line-numbers-rows,\n  .command-line .command-line-prompt {\n    border-right-color: hsla(220, 14%, 71%, 0.15);\n  }\n\n  /* Stuff in the gutter */\n  .line-numbers .line-numbers-rows > span:before,\n  .command-line .command-line-prompt > span:before {\n    color: hsl(220, 14%, 45%);\n  }\n\n  /* Match Braces plugin overrides */\n  /* Note: Outline colour is inherited from the braces */\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-1,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-5,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-9 {\n    color: hsl(355, 65%, 65%);\n  }\n\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-2,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-6,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-10 {\n    color: hsl(95, 38%, 62%);\n  }\n\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-3,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-7,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-11 {\n    color: hsl(207, 82%, 66%);\n  }\n\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-4,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-8,\n  .rainbow-braces .token.token.punctuation.brace-level-12 {\n    color: hsl(286, 60%, 67%);\n  }\n\n  /* Diff Highlight plugin overrides */\n  /* Taken from https://github.com/atom/github/blob/master/styles/variables.less */\n  pre.diff-highlight > code .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix),\n  pre > code.diff-highlight .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix) {\n    background-color: hsla(353, 100%, 66%, 0.15);\n  }\n\n  pre.diff-highlight > code .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix)::-moz-selection,\n  pre.diff-highlight > code .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix) *::-moz-selection,\n  pre > code.diff-highlight .token.token.deleted:not(.prefix)::-moz-selection,\n  pre\n    > code.diff-highlight\n .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix) {\n    background-color: hsla(137, 100%, 55%, 0.15);\n  }\n\n  pre.diff-highlight > code .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix)::-moz-selection,\n  pre.diff-highlight\n .token.token.inserted:not(.prefix) *::selection {\n    background-color: hsla(135, 73%, 55%, 0.25);\n  }\n\n  /* Previewers plugin overrides */\n  /* Based on https://github.com/atom-community/atom-ide-datatip/blob/master/styles/atom-ide-datatips.less and https://github.com/atom/atom/blob/master/packages/one-dark-ui */\n  /* Border around popup */\n  .prism-previewer.prism-previewer:before,\n  .prism-previewer-gradient.prism-previewer-gradient div {\n    border-color: hsl(224, 13%, 17%);\n  }\n\n  /* Angle and time should remain as circles and are hence not included */\n  .prism-previewer-color.prism-previewer-color:before,\n  .prism-previewer-gradient.prism-previewer-gradient div,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing:before {\n    border-radius: 0.3em;\n  }\n\n  /* Triangles pointing to the code */\n  .prism-previewer.prism-previewer:after {\n    border-top-color: hsl(224, 13%, 17%);\n  }\n\n  .prism-previewer-flipped.prism-previewer-flipped.after {\n    border-bottom-color: hsl(224, 13%, 17%);\n  }\n\n  /* Background colour within the popup */\n  .prism-previewer-angle.prism-previewer-angle:before,\n  .prism-previewer-time.prism-previewer-time:before,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing {\n    background: hsl(219, 13%, 22%);\n  }\n\n  /* For angle, this is the positive area (eg. 90deg will display one quadrant in this colour) */\n  /* For time, this is the alternate colour */\n  .prism-previewer-angle.prism-previewer-angle circle,\n  .prism-previewer-time.prism-previewer-time circle {\n    stroke: hsl(220, 14%, 71%);\n    stroke-opacity: 1;\n  }\n\n  /* Stroke colours of the handle, direction point, and vector itself */\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing circle,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing path,\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing line {\n    stroke: hsl(220, 14%, 71%);\n  }\n\n  /* Fill colour of the handle */\n  .prism-previewer-easing.prism-previewer-easing circle {\n    fill: transparent;\n  }\n}\n\ntr[data-comment-highlight=\"true\"] {\n  @apply !bg-[var(--color-yellow)]/20;\n  transition: background-color 150ms ease;\n}\n\n.dark tr[data-comment-highlight=\"true\"] {\n  @apply !bg-[var(--color-yellow)]/20;\n  background-color: rgba(188, 133,     "detailed_description": "This CSS component implements a comprehensive syntax highlighting system based on Prism.js with dual theme support (light and dark modes). It provides styling for code blocks, inline code, and specialized visual treatments for diff highlighting, comment highlighting, and various programming language syntax tokens. The component supports multiple Prism.js plugins including line highlighting, toolbar, line numbers, command line, match braces, and previewers. It also includes custom styling for comment and draft highlighting in table rows.",
    "interfaces": [],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Provide syntax highlighting for code blocks using Prism.js color schemes",
      "Implement dual theme support (light/dark) for code visualization",
      "Style diff highlighting for inserted and deleted code sections",
      "Apply visual highlighting for comments and draft annotations in diff tables",
      "Support various Prism.js plugin integrations with custom styling"
    ]
  },

    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "widget",
      "description": null,
      "file_path": "web/src/components/ui/diff/lazy.tsx",
      "functions": [
        "loadDiffModule",
        "LazyDiff",
        "LazyHunk"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "LazyDiff",
        "LazyHunk",
        "loadDiffModule"
      ],
      "name": "lazy.tsx",
      "source_summary": "import { lazy } from \"react\";\nimport type { ComponentType } from \"react\";\nimport type { DiffProps } from \"./index\";\nimport type { Hunk as HunkType, SkipBlock } from \"./utils\";\n\ntype DiffModule = typeof import(\"./index\");\n\nlet diffModulePromise: Promise<DiffModule> | null = null;\n\nconst loadDiffModule = async (): Promise<DiffModule> => {\n  if (!diffModulePromise) {\n    diffModulePromise = import(\"./index\");\n  }\n  return diffModulePromise;\n};\n\nexport const LazyDiff = lazy(\n  async (): Promise<{ default: ComponentType<DiffProps> }> => {\n    const module = await loadDiffModule();\n    return { default: module.Diff };\n  },\n);\n\nexport const LazyHunk = lazy(\n  async (): Promise<{\n    default: ComponentType<{ hunk: HunkType | SkipBlock }>;\n  }> => {\n    const module = await loadDiffModule();\n    return { default: module.Hunk };\n  },\n);\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 2.0,
      "lines_of_code": 31,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 3
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,

        "name": "react",
        "path": "react",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 2,
        "name": "ComponentType",
        "path": "react",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,

        "path": "./index",

      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 4,
        "name": "HunkType",
        "path": "./utils",
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This component implements lazy loading for diff visualization components (Diff and Hunk) in a React application. It provides asynchronous loading capabilities using React's lazy function to optimize bundle size and improve initial load performance. The component implements a singleton pattern for module loading to ensure the diff module is loaded only once and cached for subsequent requests.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Lazy-loaded Diff component for displaying unified diff content",
        "interface_type": "function",
        "name": "LazyDiff",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": "ComponentType<DiffProps>",
        "visibility": "export"
      },

        "parameters": [],

        "visibility": "export"
      },

        "description": "Internal function to load and cache the diff module using singleton pattern",

        "name": "loadDiffModule",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": "Promise<DiffModule>",
        "visibility": "private"
      }
    ],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Implement lazy loading for diff visualization components",
      "Manage asynchronous module loading with singleton pattern",

      "name": "kbd.tsx",
      "source_summary": "import { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\"\n\nfunction Kbd({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<\"kbd\">) {\n  return (\n    <kbd\n      data-slot=\"kbd\"\n      className={cn(\n        \"bg-muted text-muted-foreground pointer-events-none inline-flex h-5 w-fit min-w-5 items-center justify-center gap-1 rounded-sm px-1 font-sans text-xs font-medium select-none\",\n        \"[&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3\",\n        \"[[data-slot=tooltip-content]_&]:bg-background/20 [[data-slot=tooltip-content]_&]:text-background dark:[[data-slot=tooltip-content]_&]:bg-background/10\",\n        className\n      )}\n      {...props}\n    />\n  )\n}\n\nfunction KbdGroup({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<\"div\">) {\n  return (\n    <kbd\n      data-slot=\"kbd-group\"\n      className={cn(\"inline-flex items-center gap-1\", className)}\n      {...props}\n    />\n  )\n}\n\nexport { Kbd, KbdGroup }\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 28,

        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "The kbd.tsx component is a React UI component library module that provides keyboard key visualization elements. It consists of two main functions: Kbd - a single keyboard key component that renders semantic <kbd> HTML elements with consistent styling for keyboard shortcuts display, and KbdGroup - a container component for grouping multiple keyboard keys together. The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling and provides a clean, accessible interface for displaying keyboard shortcuts in user interfaces.",

      "name": "separator.tsx",
      "source_summary": "import * as React from \"react\";\nimport * as SeparatorPrimitive from \"@radix-ui/react-separator\";\n\nimport { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\";\n\nfunction Separator({\n  className,\n  orientation = \"horizontal\",\n  decorative = true,\n  ...props\n}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SeparatorPrimitive.Root>) {\n  return (\n    <SeparatorPrimitive.Root\n      data-slot=\"separator\"\n      decorative={decorative}\n      orientation={orientation}\n      className={cn(\n        \"bg-border shrink-0 data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:w-full data-[orientation=vertical]:h-full data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px\",\n        className,\n      )}\n      {...props}\n    />\n  );\n}\n\nexport { Separator };\n"
    },

    "detailed_description": "The Separator component is a UI component built on top of Radix UI's SeparatorPrimitive. It provides a customizable visual divider that can be used to separate content in both horizontal and vertical layouts. The component accepts orientation (horizontal/vertical), decorative properties, and custom className for styling. It uses CSS data attributes for orientation-specific styling and integrates with the project's utility class system through the cn function.",

      "description": "Theme toggle button component for switching between light and dark modes with smooth transitions",
      "file_path": "web/src/components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx",

      "name": "theme-toggle.tsx",
      "source_summary": "import { MoonIcon, SunIcon } from \"lucide-react\";\n\nimport { useTheme } from \"@/hooks/use-theme\";\nimport { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\";\n\nimport { Button } from \"./button\";\n\ntype ThemeToggleProps = {\n  className?: string;\n};\n\nexport function ThemeToggle({ className }: ThemeToggleProps) {\n  const { theme, toggleThemeWithTransition } = useTheme();\n  const isDark = theme === \"dark\";\n\n  return (\n    <Button\n      aria-label={isDark ? \"Switch to light mode\" : \"Switch to dark mode\"}\n      className={cn(\n        \"size-9 p-0 text-foreground hover:text-foreground dark:hover:text-foreground hover:bg-accent/20 dark:hover:bg-accent/20\",\n        \"cursor-pointer\",\n        className,\n      )}\n      onClick={(e) => {\n        toggleThemeWithTransition(e);\n      }}\n      size=\"icon\"\n      variant=\"outline\"\n    >\n      {isDark ? (\n        <SunIcon className=\"size-4\" />\n      ) : (\n        <MoonIcon className=\"size-4\" />\n      )}\n    </Button>\n  );\n}\n"
    },

    "detailed_description": "This is a React component that provides a theme toggle functionality for switching between light and dark modes. It renders a button that displays either a sun icon (for light mode) or moon icon (for dark mode) based on the current theme state. The component uses the useTheme hook to manage theme state and provides accessibility features through ARIA labels. It includes smooth transitions when toggling themes and follows React best practices with proper TypeScript typing.",

      "source_summary": "\"use client\";\n\nimport { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\";\nimport * as CollapsiblePrimitive from \"@radix-ui/react-collapsible\";\n\nfunction Collapsible({\n  ...props\n}: React.ComponentProps<typeof CollapsiblePrimitive.Root>) {\n  return <CollapsiblePrimitive.Root data-slot=\"collapsible\" {...props} />;\n}\n\nfunction CollapsibleTrigger({\n  ...props\n}: React.ComponentProps<typeof CollapsiblePrimitive.CollapsibleTrigger>) {\n  return (\n    <CollapsiblePrimitive.CollapsibleTrigger\n      data-slot=\"collapsible-trigger\"\n      {...props}\n      className={cn(props.className, \"cursor-pointer\")}\n    />\n  );\n}\n\nfunction CollapsibleContent({\n  ...props\n}: React.ComponentProps<typeof CollapsiblePrimitive.CollapsibleContent>) {\n  return (\n    <CollapsiblePrimitive.CollapsibleContent\n      data-slot=\"collapsible-content\"\n      {...props}\n    />\n  );\n}\n\nexport { Collapsible, CollapsibleTrigger, CollapsibleContent };\n"
    },

    "detailed_description": "This component is a wrapper implementation around Radix UI's collapsible primitive, providing a clean React interface for creating expandable/collapsible content sections. It consists of three main subcomponents: Collapsible (the container), CollapsibleTrigger (the clickable element that toggles visibility), and CollapsibleContent (the expandable content area). The implementation leverages TypeScript for type safety and includes utility class name merging using the 'cn' utility function. The component is designed as a client-side only component ('use client') and includes data-slot attributes for styling and testing purposes.",

      "source_summary": "import * as React from \"react\";\n\nimport { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\";\n\nfunction Textarea({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<\"textarea\">) {\n  return (\n    <textarea\n      data-slot=\"textarea\"\n      className={cn(\n        \"border-input placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/50 aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 aria-invalid:border-destructive dark:bg-input/30 flex field-sizing-content min-h-16 w-full rounded-md border bg-transparent px-3 py-2 text-base shadow-xs transition-[color,box-shadow] outline-none focus-visible:ring-[3px] disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 md:text-sm\",\n        className,\n      )}\n      {...props}\n    />\n  );\n}\n\nexport { Textarea };\n"

      "source_summary": "import * as React from \"react\";\n\nimport { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\";\n\nfunction Input({ className, type, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<\"input\">) {\n  return (\n    <input\n      type={type}\n      data-slot=\"input\"\n      className={cn(\n        \"file:text-foreground placeholder:text-muted-foreground selection:bg-primary selection:text-primary-foreground dark:bg-input/30 border-input h-9 w-full min-w-0 rounded-md border bg-transparent px-3 py-1 text-base shadow-xs transition-[color,box-shadow] outline-none file:inline-flex file:h-7 file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 md:text-sm\",\n        \"focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:ring-[3px]\",\n        \"aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 aria-invalid:border-destructive\",\n        className,\n      )}\n      {...props}\n    />\n  );\n}\n\nexport { Input };\n"

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "A utility library providing CSS class name merging and text normalization functions",
      "file_path": "web/src/lib/utils.ts",
      "functions": [
        "cn",
        "shortenTitle"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [],
      "name": "utils.ts",
      "source_summary": "import { clsx, type ClassValue } from \"clsx\";\nimport { twMerge } from \"tailwind-merge\";\n\nexport function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {\n  return twMerge(clsx(inputs));\n}\n\n/**\n * Normalize and shorten a title string.\n * - Replaces multiple whitespace with single space\n * - Trims leading/trailing whitespace\n * - Shortens to maxLength characters with ellipsis\n */\nexport function shortenTitle(\n  title: string | undefined | null,\n  maxLength = 50,\n): string {\n  if (!title) return \"\";\n\n  // Normalize: collapse whitespace and trim\n  const normalized = title.replace(/\\s+/g, \" \").trim();\n\n  if (normalized.length <= maxLength) {\n    return normalized;\n  }\n\n  // Shorten with ellipsis\n  return `${normalized.slice(0, maxLength - 1)}…`;\n}\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 3.0,
      "lines_of_code": 29,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 2
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "library",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 1,
        "name": "clsx",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "library",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 2,
        "name": "tailwind-merge",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This utility component provides two core functions: 1) `cn()` function that intelligently merges Tailwind CSS classes using clsx and twMerge libraries, handling conditional classes and preventing conflicts; 2) `shortenTitle()` function that normalizes text strings by collapsing whitespace, trimming, and shortening with ellipsis for display purposes. The component serves as a foundational utility layer for the frontend application.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Merges CSS class names using clsx and twMerge to handle conditional classes and prevent conflicts",
        "interface_type": "function",
        "name": "cn",
        "parameters": [
          {
            "description": "Array of CSS class values to merge",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "inputs",
            "param_type": "ClassValue[]"
          }
        ],
        "return_type": "string",
        "visibility": "exported"
      },

        "description": "Normalizes whitespace, trims, and shortens title strings with ellipsis for UI display",

        "parameters": [

            "description": "Title string to normalize and shorten",
            "is_optional": false,

          },

  {
    "code_dossier": {

      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport importlib\nimport sys\n\n# Alias the kimi_code package to kimi_cli for compatibility.\nsys.modules[__name__] = importlib.import_module(\"kimi_cli\")\n"

    "detailed_description": "This is a lightweight compatibility shim module that creates a package alias from `kimi_code` to `kimi_cli`. The module dynamically replaces itself in Python's module registry (sys.modules) with the actual `kimi_cli` module upon import. This clever mechanism ensures seamless backward compatibility for codebases that still reference the old `kimi_code` package name after a package rename. The implementation uses Python's dynamic module system to achieve transparent redirection without requiring any code changes in dependent modules.",

    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "context",
      "description": "Context management module that provides context-aware access to Kaos instances using Python's ContextVar mechanism for async-safe state management",

      "functions": [],
      "importance_score": 0.6,

        "current_kaos"
      ],
      "name": "_current.py",
      "source_summary": "from contextvars import ContextVar\n\nfrom kaos import Kaos\nfrom kaos.local import local_kaos\n\ncurrent_kaos = ContextVar[Kaos](\"current_kaos\", default=local_kaos)\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 6,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 0
    },

      {

        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 1,

        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 3,
        "name": "Kaos",
        "path": "kaos",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 3,
        "name": "local_kaos",
        "path": "kaos.local",
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This component provides a context-local variable for managing the current Kaos instance in a thread-safe and coroutine-safe manner. It uses Python's contextvars.ContextVar to create a context-aware storage mechanism that maintains separate values for each execution context (thread or coroutine). The component defines a single ContextVar named 'current_kaos' with a default value of 'local_kaos', ensuring that when no explicit context is set, the system falls back to a local Kaos instance. This pattern is essential for frameworks that need to maintain per-request or per-task state across async boundaries, making it suitable for web applications, async task processing, and other concurrent execution scenarios.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "A ContextVar[Kaos] that stores and retrieves the current Kaos instance for the active execution context. Supports get() and set() operations and defaults to local_kaos when not explicitly set.",
        "interface_type": "context_variable",
        "name": "current_kaos",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": "ContextVar[Kaos]",
        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Provide context-aware storage for Kaos instances across async boundaries",
      "Enable thread-safe and coroutine-safe access to current Kaos instance",
      "Offer default fallback to local_kaos when context is not explicitly set",
      "Serve as the central accessor point for current Kaos context throughout the application"
    ]
  },
  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "tool",
      "description": "A Null Object Pattern implementation of Toolset that contains no tools and returns ToolNotFoundError for all tool calls",
      "file_path": "packages/kosong/src/kosong/tooling/empty.py",
      "functions": [
        "tools",
        "handle",
        "type_check"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "Toolset"
      ],
      "name": "empty.py",
      "source_summary": "from typing import TYPE_CHECKING\n\nfrom kosong.message import ToolCall\nfrom kosong.tooling import HandleResult, Tool, ToolResult, Toolset\nfrom kosong.tooling.error import ToolNotFoundError\n\nif TYPE_CHECKING:\n\n    def type_check(empty: \"EmptyToolset\"):\n        _: Toolset = empty\n\n\nclass EmptyToolset:\n    \"\"\"A toolset implementation that always contains no tools.\"\"\"\n\n    @property\n    def tools(self) -> list[Tool]:\n        return []\n\n    def handle(self, tool_call: ToolCall) -> HandleResult:\n        return ToolResult(\n            tool_call_id=tool_call.id,\n            return_value=ToolNotFoundError(tool_call.function.name),\n        )\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 2.0,

      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This component implements an EmptyToolset class that serves as a null object pattern implementation for the Toolset interface in the Kosong framework. It provides a toolset that always contains zero tools and handles all incoming tool calls by returning a structured error result indicating that no tool was found. The class includes a type-checking function to verify interface compliance at type-check time. This implementation is useful as a default toolset, a fallback handler when no tools are available, or as a stub for testing scenarios where tool functionality is not required.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Returns an empty list representing no available tools in this toolset",

        "name": "tools",
        "parameters": [],

      },

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "Asynchronous utility function for handling both synchronous and asynchronous callbacks",
      "file_path": "packages/kosong/src/kosong/utils/aio.py",
      "functions": [
        "callback"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [],
      "name": "aio.py",
      "source_summary": "import inspect\nfrom collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable\nfrom typing import cast\n\ntype Callback[**Params, Return] = Callable[Params, Awaitable[Return] | Return]\n\n\nasync def callback[**Params, Return](\n    fn: Callback[Params, Return], *args: Params.args, **kwargs: Params.kwargs\n) -> Return:\n    ret = fn(*args, **kwargs)\n    if inspect.isawaitable(ret):\n        return await cast(Awaitable[Return], ret)\n    return ret\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 2.0,
      "lines_of_code": 14,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 1
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 1,
        "name": "inspect",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 2,
        "name": "collections.abc",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 3,
        "name": "typing",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This component provides a generic asynchronous utility function that can handle both synchronous and asynchronous callbacks transparently. The `callback` function takes any callable (function, method, or lambda) and intelligently determines whether the return value is awaitable. If it's an awaitable (coroutine), it awaits the result; if it's a regular synchronous return value, it returns it directly. This allows developers to write code that works seamlessly with both sync and async functions without needing to handle the differences explicitly.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Executes a callback function and handles both synchronous and asynchronous returns transparently",
        "interface_type": "function",
        "name": "callback",
        "parameters": [
          {
            "description": "The callback function to execute, which can be either synchronous or asynchronous",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "fn",
            "param_type": "Callback[Params, Return]"
          },
          {
            "description": "Positional arguments to pass to the callback function",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "args",
            "param_type": "Params.args"
          },
          {
            "description": "Keyword arguments to pass to the callback function",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "kwargs",
            "param_type": "Params.kwargs"
          }
        ],
        "return_type": "Return",
        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Provide transparent handling of both synchronous and asynchronous callbacks",
      "Abstract away the complexity of distinguishing between sync and async function calls",
      "Ensure type safety when working with mixed sync/async codebases",
      "Serve as a utility wrapper for callbacks in asynchronous contexts"
    ]
  },

    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "Rich console configuration module with neutral markdown theme for CLI applications",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/ui/shell/console.py",

        "console initialization",

      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "console"
      ],
      "name": "console.py",
      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom rich.console import Console\nfrom rich.theme import Theme\n\n_NEUTRAL_MARKDOWN_THEME = Theme(\n    {\n        \"markdown.paragraph\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.block_quote\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.hr\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.item\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.item.bullet\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.item.number\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.link\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.link_url\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.h1\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.h1.border\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.h2\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.h3\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.h4\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.h5\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.h6\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.em\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.strong\": \"none\",\n        \"markdown.s\": \"none\",\n        \"status.spinner\": \"none\",\n    },\n    inherit=True,\n)\n\nconsole = Console(highlight=False, theme=_NEUTRAL_MARKDOWN_THEME)\n"
    },

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "Custom exception hierarchy module for Kimi CLI application, providing domain-specific exception classes for configuration, agent specification, tool validation, and MCP-related errors",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/exception.py",
      "functions": [],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "KimiCLIException",
        "ConfigError",
        "AgentSpecError",
        "InvalidToolError",
        "SystemPromptTemplateError",
        "MCPConfigError",
        "MCPRuntimeError"
      ],
      "name": "exception.py",
      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\n\nclass KimiCLIException(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception class for Kimi Code CLI.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\nclass ConfigError(KimiCLIException, ValueError):\n    \"\"\"Configuration error.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\nclass AgentSpecError(KimiCLIException, ValueError):\n    \"\"\"Agent specification error.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\nclass InvalidToolError(KimiCLIException, ValueError):\n    \"\"\"Invalid tool error.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\nclass SystemPromptTemplateError(KimiCLIException, ValueError):\n    \"\"\"System prompt template error.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\nclass MCPConfigError(KimiCLIException, ValueError):\n    \"\"\"MCP config error.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\nclass MCPRuntimeError(KimiCLIException, RuntimeError):\n    \"\"\"MCP runtime error.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 2.0,
      "lines_of_code": 43,
      "number_of_classes": 7,
      "number_of_functions": 0
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 1,
        "name": "__future__.annotations",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This module establishes a structured exception hierarchy for the Kimi CLI application. It defines a base KimiCLIException class and six specialized exception types organized by functional domain: configuration errors (ConfigError, MCPConfigError), specification/validation errors (AgentSpecError, InvalidToolError, SystemPromptTemplateError), and runtime errors (MCPRuntimeError). The design uses multiple inheritance with Python's built-in ValueError and RuntimeError to ensure both application-specific catch handling and standard Python exception semantics. This enables fine-grained error handling while maintaining compatibility with Python's exception handling patterns.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Base exception class for Kimi Code CLI, inherits from Exception",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "KimiCLIException",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "Configuration error, inherits from KimiCLIException and ValueError",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "ConfigError",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "Agent specification error, inherits from KimiCLIException and ValueError",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "AgentSpecError",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "Invalid tool error, inherits from KimiCLIException and ValueError",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "InvalidToolError",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "System prompt template error, inherits from KimiCLIException and ValueError",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "SystemPromptTemplateError",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "MCP config error, inherits from KimiCLIException and ValueError",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "MCPConfigError",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "MCP runtime error, inherits from KimiCLIException and RuntimeError",

        "name": "MCPRuntimeError",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],
    "responsibilities": [

      "Provide domain-specific exception types for configuration, agent, tool, and MCP errors",

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "tool",
      "description": "A functional tool component implementing a Think tool that accepts a thought string parameter and returns a logging acknowledgment. Part of the kimi_cli tooling ecosystem, following the CallableTool2 framework pattern.",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/tools/think/__init__.py",
      "functions": [
        "__call__"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "Params",
        "Think",
        "__call__"
      ],
      "name": "__init__.py",
      "source_summary": "from pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import override\n\nfrom kosong.tooling import CallableTool2, ToolOk, ToolReturnValue\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field\n\nfrom kimi_cli.tools.utils import load_desc\n\n\nclass Params(BaseModel):\n    thought: str = Field(description=(\"A thought to think about.\"))\n\n\nclass Think(CallableTool2[Params]):\n    name: str = \"Think\"\n    description: str = load_desc(Path(__file__).parent / \"think.md\", {})\n    params: type[Params] = Params\n\n    @override\n    async def __call__(self, params: Params) -> ToolReturnValue:\n        return ToolOk(output=\"\", message=\"Thought logged\")\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 21,
      "number_of_classes": 2,
      "number_of_functions": 1
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 1,
        "name": "pathlib.Path",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 2,
        "name": "typing.override",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 4,
        "name": "kosong.tooling",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 5,
        "name": "pydantic",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 7,
        "name": "kimi_cli.tools.utils",
        "path": "src/kimi_cli/tools/utils",
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This is a Think Tool component that implements a lightweight thought-logging mechanism within a CLI tooling framework. The component follows the CallableTool2 pattern from the kosong.tooling framework, designed as an executable tool in an AI or automation system.\n\nThe tool accepts a single string parameter (thought) via the Params Pydantic model, loads its description dynamically from an external markdown file (think.md), and returns a ToolOk response with empty output and a confirmation message 'Thought logged'. The implementation is intentionally minimal, serving as a standardized interface for recording or processing thoughts in the system, though actual thought processing logic appears to be placeholder or delegated elsewhere.\n\nThe component demonstrates clean separation of concerns with parameter validation, tool registration through the framework interface, and external configuration loading for documentation.\"",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Pydantic model for validating Think tool parameters with a 'thought' string field",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "Params",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "Main tool class implementing CallableTool2 interface for thought processing functionality",
        "interface_type": "class",
        "name": "Think",
        "parameters": [],
        "return_type": null,
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "Async method that processes the thought parameter and returns a ToolOk acknowledgment",
        "interface_type": "method",
        "name": "__call__",
        "parameters": [
          {
            "description": "The validated parameters containing the thought to process",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "params",
            "param_type": "Params"
          }
        ],

        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],

      "name": "__init__.py",
      "source_summary": "from enum import StrEnum\n\n\nclass FileOpsWindow:\n    \"\"\"Maintains a window of file operations.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\nclass FileActions(StrEnum):\n    READ = \"read file\"\n    EDIT = \"edit file\"\n    EDIT_OUTSIDE = \"edit file outside of working directory\"\n\n\nfrom .glob import Glob  # noqa: E402\nfrom .grep_local import Grep  # noqa: E402\nfrom .read import ReadFile  # noqa: E402\nfrom .read_media import ReadMediaFile  # noqa: E402\nfrom .replace import StrReplaceFile  # noqa: E402\nfrom .write import WriteFile  # noqa: E402\n\n__all__ = (\n    \"ReadFile\",\n    \"ReadMediaFile\",\n    \"Glob\",\n    \"Grep\",\n    \"WriteFile\",\n    \"StrReplaceFile\",\n)\n"
    },

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "types",
      "description": "Display block type definitions for kimi_cli tool, providing structured data models for rendering diff content, todo lists, and shell commands",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/tools/display.py",
      "functions": [],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "DiffDisplayBlock",
        "TodoDisplayItem",
        "TodoDisplayBlock",
        "ShellDisplayBlock"
      ],
      "name": "display.py",
      "source_summary": "from typing import Literal\n\nfrom kosong.tooling import DisplayBlock\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\n\n\nclass DiffDisplayBlock(DisplayBlock):\n    \"\"\"Display block describing a file diff.\"\"\"\n\n    type: str = \"diff\"\n    path: str\n    old_text: str\n    new_text: str\n\n\nclass TodoDisplayItem(BaseModel):\n    title: str\n    status: Literal[\"pending\", \"in_progress\", \"done\"]\n\n\nclass TodoDisplayBlock(DisplayBlock):\n    \"\"\"Display block describing a todo list update.\"\"\"\n\n    type: str = \"todo\"\n    items: list[TodoDisplayItem]\n\n\nclass ShellDisplayBlock(DisplayBlock):\n    \"\"\"Display block describing a shell command.\"\"\"\n\n    type: str = \"shell\"\n    language: str\n    command: str\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 33,
      "number_of_classes": 4,
      "number_of_functions": 0
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "Literal",
        "path": "typing",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "DisplayBlock",
        "path": "kosong.tooling",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "BaseModel",
        "path": "pydantic",
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This module defines structured data models for display blocks used in the kimi_cli application. It extends the kosong.tooling DisplayBlock base class to create specialized display types: DiffDisplayBlock for file diff visualization with path and text comparison data, TodoDisplayBlock for todo list presentation with items containing title and status information, and ShellDisplayBlock for shell command display with language identification. All models leverage Pydantic for data validation and serialization, providing type-safe interfaces for the display rendering system.\"",
    "interfaces": [

        "description": "Display block for file diff visualization, contains path and old/new text comparison data",

      },

        "parameters": [],

        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],

    "detailed_description": "This is a minimal configuration file that defines wire protocol version constants for the kimi-cli project's communication layer. The file declares two string constants: WIRE_PROTOCOL_VERSION (current version \"1.3\") and WIRE_PROTOCOL_LEGACY_VERSION (legacy version \"1.1\"). These constants serve as a single source of truth for protocol versioning across the wire communication module, enabling consistent version checking, compatibility validation, and protocol negotiation throughout the system. The file follows a simple constants pattern with type annotations, positioned at the foundational layer of the application architecture.",

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "Serialization and deserialization utilities for wire messages in the Kimi CLI system",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/wire/serde.py",
      "functions": [
        "serialize_wire_message",
        "deserialize_wire_message"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "serialize_wire_message",
        "deserialize_wire_message"
      ],
      "name": "serde.py",
      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom kosong.utils.typing import JsonType\n\nfrom kimi_cli.wire.types import WireMessage, WireMessageEnvelope\n\n\ndef serialize_wire_message(msg: WireMessage) -> dict[str, JsonType]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert a `WireMessage` into a jsonifiable dict.\n    \"\"\"\n    envelope = WireMessageEnvelope.from_wire_message(msg)\n    return envelope.model_dump(mode=\"json\")\n\n\ndef deserialize_wire_message(data: dict[str, JsonType] | Any) -> WireMessage:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert a jsonifiable dict into a `WireMessage`.\n\n    Raises:\n        ValueError: If the message type is unknown or the payload is invalid.\n    \"\"\"\n    envelope = WireMessageEnvelope.model_validate(data)\n    return envelope.to_wire_message()\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 26,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 2
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 3,
        "name": "typing",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "third_party",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 5,
        "name": "kosong.utils.typing",
        "path": "kosong.utils.typing",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "internal",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 7,
        "name": "kimi_cli.wire.types",
        "path": "kimi_cli.wire.types",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "type",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 5,
        "name": "JsonType",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This component provides serialization and deserialization functionality for wire messages in the Kimi CLI system. It acts as a bridge between the application's internal WireMessage objects and JSON-serializable dictionaries. The serialize_wire_message function converts WireMessage instances into JSON-serializable dictionaries using WireMessageEnvelope as an intermediate format, while deserialize_wire_message performs the reverse operation, converting JSON data back into WireMessage instances with proper validation and error handling.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Convert a WireMessage into a jsonifiable dict",
        "interface_type": "function",
        "name": "serialize_wire_message",
        "parameters": [
          {
            "description": "The wire message to serialize",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "msg",
            "param_type": "WireMessage"
          }
        ],
        "return_type": "dict[str, JsonType]",
        "visibility": "public"
      },
      {
        "description": "Convert a jsonifiable dict into a WireMessage, raises ValueError for unknown message types or invalid payloads",
        "interface_type": "function",
        "name": "deserialize_wire_message",
        "parameters": [
          {
            "description": "The JSON data to deserialize",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "data",
            "param_type": "dict[str, JsonType] | Any"
          }
        ],
        "return_type": "WireMessage",
        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Provide serialization of WireMessage objects to JSON-serializable dictionaries",
      "Provide deserialization of JSON data back to WireMessage objects",
      "Handle validation and error checking during deserialization",
      "Maintain type safety between internal message objects and external data formats",
      "Act as an adapter between application domain objects and serialization formats"
    ]
  },

      "source_summary": "\"\"\"API routes.\"\"\"\n\nfrom kimi_cli.web.api import config, open_in, sessions\n\nconfig_router = config.router\nsessions_router = sessions.router\nwork_dirs_router = sessions.work_dirs_router\nopen_in_router = open_in.router\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"config_router\",\n    \"open_in_router\",\n    \"sessions_router\",\n    \"work_dirs_router\",\n]\n"

    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "config",
      "description": "Package initializer that configures Loguru logging behavior for the kimi_cli package by disabling logging by default for library usage.",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/__init__.py",
      "functions": [],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [],
      "name": "__init__.py",
      "source_summary": "from loguru import logger\n\n# Disable logging by default for library usage.\n# Application entry points (e.g., kimi_cli.cli) should call logger.enable(\"kimi_cli\")\n# to enable logging.\nlogger.disable(\"kimi_cli\")\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 2.0,
      "lines_of_code": 6,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 0
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "import",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 1,
        "name": "loguru",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This package-level initializer imports Loguru's global `logger` and immediately disables logging for the `kimi_cli` namespace via `logger.disable(\"kimi_cli\")`. The intent is to keep the library quiet when imported as a dependency, requiring explicit opt-in by application entry points (e.g., `kimi_cli.cli`) to enable logging using `logger.enable(\"kimi_cli\")`. The file contains no callable functions or classes; its behavior executes on import, providing side-effect-based configuration at package import time.",
    "interfaces": [],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Establish default logging policy for the `kimi_cli` package (disabled on import).",
      "Prevent unsolicited log output when the package is used as a library dependency.",
      "Document the intended mechanism for applications/entry points to enable logs (`logger.enable(\"kimi_cli\")`).",
      "Provide a single, centralized location for package-wide logging configuration defaults."
    ]
  },

    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "Utility module providing secure aiohttp client session creation with SSL certificate verification",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/utils/aiohttp.py",
      "functions": [
        "new_client_session"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "new_client_session"
      ],
      "name": "aiohttp.py",
      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport ssl\n\nimport aiohttp\nimport certifi\n\n_ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())\n\n\ndef new_client_session() -> aiohttp.ClientSession:\n    return aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=aiohttp.TCPConnector(ssl=_ssl_context))\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 12,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 1
    },

      },

        "dependency_type": "external_library",

        "line_number": 6,
        "name": "certifi",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This component is a utility module that provides a factory function for creating secure aiohttp ClientSession instances. The core functionality involves creating an SSL context with system CA certificates and then using this context to instantiate aiohttp client sessions. This ensures that all HTTP requests made through sessions created by this module use proper SSL certificate verification, preventing potential security vulnerabilities from unverified SSL connections.",

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "Utility module providing atomic JSON file writing functionality to prevent data corruption",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/utils/io.py",
      "functions": [
        "atomic_json_write"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "atomic_json_write"
      ],
      "name": "io.py",
      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport contextlib\nimport json\nimport os\nimport tempfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\n\ndef atomic_json_write(data: Any, path: Path) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write JSON data to a file atomically using tmp-file + os.replace.\n\n    This prevents data corruption if the process crashes mid-write: either the\n    old file is kept intact or the new file is fully committed.\n    \"\"\"\n    fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=path.parent, suffix=\".tmp\")\n    try:\n        with os.fdopen(fd, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n            f.flush()\n            os.fsync(f.fileno())\n        os.replace(tmp_path, path)\n    except BaseException:\n        with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n            os.unlink(tmp_path)\n        raise\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 2.0,
      "lines_of_code": 27,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 1
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "contextlib",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "json",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "os",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "tempfile",
        "path": null,
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "pathlib",
        "path": "Path",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "typing",
        "path": "Any",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "standard_library",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "__future__",
        "path": "annotations",
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This component provides a single utility function `atomic_json_write` that implements atomic file writing for JSON data. The function ensures data integrity by using a temporary file approach combined with os.replace operation, which guarantees that either the old file remains intact or the new file is fully committed, preventing partial writes and data corruption during system crashes or interruptions. The implementation includes proper error handling, file flushing, and fsync operations to ensure data durability.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Write JSON data to a file atomically using tmp-file + os.replace to prevent data corruption",
        "interface_type": "function",
        "name": "atomic_json_write",
        "parameters": [
          {
            "description": "JSON serializable data to write",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "data",
            "param_type": "Any"
          },
          {
            "description": "Target file path for writing",
            "is_optional": false,
            "name": "path",
            "param_type": "Path"
          }
        ],
        "return_type": "None",
        "visibility": "public"
      }
    ],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Atomic file writing operations for JSON data",
      "Data integrity protection during file writes",
      "Temporary file management and cleanup",
      "Error handling and resource cleanup",
      "Cross-platform file system operations"
    ]
  },
  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "PyInstaller hook configuration for bundling Kimi CLI application resources and dependencies",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/utils/pyinstaller.py",
      "functions": [
        "configure_pyinstaller_bundle"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [],
      "name": "pyinstaller.py",
      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom PyInstaller.utils.hooks import collect_data_files, collect_submodules\n\nhiddenimports = collect_submodules(\"kimi_cli.tools\") + [\"setproctitle\"]\ndatas = (\n    collect_data_files(\n        \"kimi_cli\",\n        includes=[\n            \"agents/**/*.yaml\",\n            \"agents/**/*.md\",\n            \"deps/bin/**\",\n            \"prompts/**/*.md\",\n            \"skills/**\",\n            \"tools/**/*.md\",\n            \"web/static/**\",\n            \"CHANGELOG.md\",\n        ],\n        excludes=[\n            \"tools/*.md\",\n        ],\n    )\n    + collect_data_files(\n        \"dateparser\",\n        includes=[\"**/*.pkl\"],\n    )\n    + collect_data_files(\n        \"fastmcp\",\n        includes=[\"../fastmcp-*.dist-info/*\"],\n    )\n)\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 1.0,
      "lines_of_code": 31,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 0
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "function",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "collect_data_files",
        "path": "PyInstaller.utils.hooks",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "function",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": null,
        "name": "collect_submodules",
        "path": "PyInstaller.utils.hooks",
        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "This PyInstaller hook configuration file defines how to bundle the Kimi CLI application into a standalone executable. It collects hidden Python imports from the tools package and setproctitle module, while also gathering various resource files including agent configurations, binary dependencies, prompt templates, skill definitions, web static assets, and external library data files. The component uses PyInstaller's utility functions to systematically include all necessary application assets while excluding redundant documentation files.",
    "interfaces": [],
    "responsibilities": [
      "Resource collection for application bundling",
      "Dependency resolution for hidden imports",
      "Bundle configuration management"
    ]
  },

  {
    "code_dossier": {
      "code_purpose": "util",
      "description": "Utility function for converting Message objects to string representations",
      "file_path": "src/kimi_cli/utils/message.py",
      "functions": [
        "message_stringify"
      ],
      "importance_score": 0.6,
      "interfaces": [
        "message_stringify"
      ],
      "name": "message.py",
      "source_summary": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom kosong.message import Message\n\nfrom kimi_cli.wire.types import AudioURLPart, ImageURLPart, TextPart, VideoURLPart\n\n\ndef message_stringify(message: Message) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Get a string representation of a message.\"\"\"\n    # TODO: this should be merged into `kosong.message.Message.extract_text`\n    parts: list[str] = []\n    for part in message.content:\n        if isinstance(part, TextPart):\n            parts.append(part.text)\n        elif isinstance(part, ImageURLPart):\n            parts.append(\"[image]\")\n        elif isinstance(part, AudioURLPart):\n            suffix = f\":{part.audio_url.id}\" if part.audio_url.id else \"\"\n            parts.append(f\"[audio{suffix}]\")\n        elif isinstance(part, VideoURLPart):\n            parts.append(\"[video]\")\n        else:\n            parts.append(f\"[{part.type}]\")\n    return \"\".join(parts)\n"
    },
    "complexity_metrics": {
      "cyclomatic_complexity": 7.0,
      "lines_of_code": 24,
      "number_of_classes": 0,
      "number_of_functions": 1
    },
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "dependency_type": "module",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 3,
        "name": "kosong.message",
        "path": "kosong.message",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "module",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 5,
        "name": "kimi_cli.wire.types",
        "path": "kimi_cli.wire.types",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "class",
        "is_external": true,
        "line_number": 7,
        "name": "Message",
        "path": "kosong.message",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "class",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 11,
        "name": "TextPart",
        "path": "kimi_cli.wire.types",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "class",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 13,

        "path": "kimi_cli.wire.types",
        "version": null
      },
      {
        "dependency_type": "class",
        "is_external": false,
        "line_number": 15,
        "name": "AudioURLPart",
        "path": "kimi_cli.wire.types",
        "version": null
      },
      {

        "version": null
      }
    ],
    "detailed_description": "The message.py component provides a utility function `message_stringify` that converts a Message object into a human-readable string representation. It processes different types of message parts (text, image, audio, video) and converts them into appropriate string tokens. For text parts, it directly appends the text content; for media parts (image, audio, video), it creates descriptive tokens like '[image]', '[audio]', '[video]'; for unknown part types, it creates a generic token with the part type. The function handles audio URLs specially by including the audio ID if available. This utility is essential for displaying message content in a simplified text format, particularly useful for logging, debugging, or text-only interfaces.",
    "interfaces": [
      {
        "description": "Converts a Message object to a string representation by processing all content parts",
